steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content:
Find the GCF of $64t^2s^3$ and $56t^3s$. The numerical GCF of 64 and 56 is 8; the variable GCF is $t^2s$ (lowest power of $t$ is 2, lowest power of $s$ is 1). The combined GCF is $8t^2s$, but the answer choices factor only $4t^2s$ or $4ts$ out, so pull out $4t^2s$:
$$64t^2s^3 - 56t^3s = 4t^2s(16s^2 - 14t)$$

steps.title: Test each choice by distributing
steps.content:
• $4ts \left(16{s}^{2}-14ts\right)$: $4ts(16s^2 - 14ts) = 64ts^3 - 56t^2s^2$ — wrong exponents on $t$.
  • $4ts \left(16{t}^{2}{s}^{2}-14t\right)$: $4ts(16t^2s^2 - 14t) = 64t^3s^3 - 56t^2s$ — wrong exponents.
  • $4{t}^{2}s \left(16ts-14s\right)$: $4t^2s(16ts - 14s) = 64t^3s^2 - 56t^2s^2$ — wrong exponents on $s$.
  • $4{t}^{2}s \left(16{s}^{2}-14t\right)$: $4t^2s(16s^2 - 14t) = 64t^2s^3 - 56t^3s$ — matches the original.
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
